methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series B), post-money $600M

data.narrative:
Science Corporation is a Series B biotech and health company founded by Max Hodak that has raised $200M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, plus 3 public biotech benchmarks.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the $200M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying $380M. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-stage, same-sector comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $810M with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure, standard for private companies. The three public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) show a sector P/S median of 6.6x but lack target application data.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $323M–$931M, with a median of $595M. The peer-median approach anchors toward the upper half given Science Corporation's stage and capital raised, while stage-progression provides downside support. Key uncertainties include competitive differentiation within the biotech peer set, the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($600M–$3.3B), and absence of revenue metrics to cross-check valuation density.
